gene_description: BRAF (B-Raf Proto-Oncogene, Serine/Threonine Kinase) encodes a component of the MAPK/ERK signaling pathway. The V600E mutation is the most common BRAF alteration, occurring in ~50% of melanomas. This constitutively active mutation drives rapid tumor growth and predicts excellent response to BRAF-directed therapies.

source.title: Inhibition of mutated, activated BRAF in metastatic melanoma
source.finding: BRAF V600E inhibition with vemurafenib achieved 81% response rate in phase 1; established BRAF as druggable target in melanoma

AA.effect: Constitutively active BRAF kinase; strong MAPK pathway activation; 81% response rate to vemurafenib
AA.recommendation: BRAF inhibitor monotherapy or combined BRAF+MEK inhibition (preferred for improved PFS and OS). Monitor for secondary cutaneous malignancies.

AT.effect: BRAF V600E present; excellent response to targeted therapy expected; constitutively active kinase
AT.recommendation: BRAF+MEK inhibitor combination preferred (dabrafenib+trametinib or encorafenib+cobimetinib). Superior to monotherapy for PFS and OS.

TA.effect: BRAF V600E present; excellent response to targeted therapy expected; constitutively active kinase
TA.recommendation: BRAF+MEK inhibitor combination preferred (dabrafenib+trametinib or encorafenib+cobimetinib). Superior to monotherapy for PFS and OS.

TT.effect: BRAF V600E not present; BRAF inhibitors not indicated; consider NRAS or other mutations
TT.recommendation: Immunotherapy (anti-PD-1/PD-L1) is first-line. Consider MEK inhibitor if NRAS mutant. Evaluate for other driver mutations.

clinical_significance: FDA companion diagnostic for vemurafenib and other BRAF inhibitors. BRAF V600E present in ~50% of melanomas. Transformed melanoma treatment from chemotherapy to precision medicine.
